description: Reusable validation rules for Studio registries, DataByteSprites indexes, runtime manifests, source ownership maps, and mechanics graphs.

validationEngine.goal: Detect broken references, malformed records, invalid runtime load order, duplicate IDs, and documentation drift before new gameplay systems are added.

ruleSets.name: Registry Availability
ruleSets.description: Every registered Knowledge Engine or diagnostics source must load, parse, declare a schemaVersion, and expose at least one expected collection record.

ruleSets.name: Required Fields
ruleSets.description: Records must expose stable IDs and readable titles so they can participate in Studio search, graph validation, and future AI tooling.

ruleSets.name: Cross-Index References
ruleSets.description: Dependencies, relationships, learnedBy fields, assignedTo fields, and graph edges should point to known Studio or DataByteSprites IDs.

ruleSets.name: Mechanics Graph Integrity
ruleSets.description: Every mechanics graph edge must reference existing graph nodes and use a declared edge type.

ruleSets.name: Runtime Load Order
ruleSets.description: Runtime modules must have unique IDs, valid dependencies, and reachable scripts before the runtime can be trusted.

ruleSets.name: Orphan Detection
ruleSets.description: Useful records should either be referenced by another system or clearly marked as planned/design-seed/future content.

nextImplementationSteps: Load this rules manifest from Diagnostics.
Build a common ID map from all sources in /studio/diagnostics/sources.json.
Validate mechanics graph edges against graph nodes.
Validate species, lore, moves, abilities, and source files against the common ID map.
Surface health score, errors, warnings, and info-level findings in the Diagnostics UI.
